name: judicial_technology_emergency_exemption_mesa_jus_mer
premise: Two departments published incompatible guidance; frontline staff are improvising and liability is concentrating on one signature line. In Judicial Technology, the situation tagged internally around judicial technology emergency exemption mesa jus mer has converged with local pressure in the Harborline corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. A union or patient advocate group is on the line demanding a written commitment before the next operational shift. An internal red-team note and a customer ticket describe the same failure mode with incompatible timelines. A public briefing is scheduled; if no coherent position exists, staff will improvise talking points from conflicting drafts. The organization’s past incidents make justice feel morally loud today, while mercy is the value that quietly erodes if you always choose expediency.

agents.name: Mei Navarro
agents.identity: I am Mei Navarro, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Judicial Technology. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat mercy as disposable collateral every time justice spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Mateo Haddad
agents.identity: I am Mateo Haddad, the risk and equity liaison pushing back on silent harms in Judicial Technology. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so justice cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes mercy in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will sequence a conservative rollout: smallest blast radius first, documented exceptions only, and real appeals paths—even if justice advocates call that foot-dragging.
I will push a fast, organization-wide lever because partial fixes may leave the worst harm untouched—even if that compresses protections for mercy.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Partial evidence is all that will exist before the window closes. Mei Navarro must choose under justice versus mercy. What does Mei Navarro do?
